location.office_time_required: four days in office, one day remote (Friday)

secondary_archetype: Fund Operations / Private Markets Product Manager

seniority.level: Senior IC to Principal (based on 6–15 years range and autonomy signals)
level_signals: Operates with a high degree of autonomy and ownership
Acts as central coordination and escalation point across complex fund structures

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6–15 years of relevant experience in product management, fund structuring, fund operations or related roles within private markets
Strong experience with Luxembourg fund structures, particularly UCI Part II, RAIFs or similar vehicles
Strong understanding of private markets fund structures, lifecycle management and governance frameworks
Familiarity with AIFM environments and key service providers (administrator, depositary, auditors)
Solid understanding of fund documentation and its operational implications
Proven ability to operate in complex, multi-stakeholder environments with a high degree of ownership
Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to handle non-standard and time-sensitive situations
High attention to detail and strong organisational capabilities
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills across internal and external parties

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with evergreen or semi-liquid structures is advantageous
Exposure to cross-border product structuring and multi-jurisdictional setups is a plus

scope.surface: Post-launch product management and lifecycle oversight for private markets funds in EMEA, primarily Luxembourg-domiciled structures (UCI Part II, RAIFs)
scope.business_model: B2B financial services platform for private markets fund distribution and operations
primary_metrics_inferred: Timely and consistent fund operational execution
Client and investor satisfaction (DDQ/query response quality)
Governance and compliance adherence across fund structures
Successful post-launch enhancements and share class additions
core_responsibilities: Act as the central coordination point across Structuring, Legal, Tax, Operations, Compliance and Platform teams
Oversee operational aspects of fund structures, including share class design, fee structures, reporting requirements and platform configuration
Coordinate the operational review and implementation of fund documentation (PPMs, LPAs, subscription materials)
Serve as the primary interface with clients, GPs and external stakeholders on operational and product-related matters
Respond to due diligence questionnaires (DDQs), investor queries and fund-related information requests
Handle AML / KYC questionnaires and coordinate with relevant internal teams
Act as escalation point for complex operational, governance and product-related issues
Lead and implement product enhancements post-launch, including structural adjustments and new share classes
Contribute to standardisation of processes, templates and service frameworks across EMEA

green_flags: Highly specific domain: Luxembourg fund structures (UCI Part II, RAIFs) — role is well-defined and not vague
Multiple office locations offered (Lisbon, Zurich, London) providing geographic flexibility within EMEA
Clear responsibilities spanning operations, governance, client interaction and post-launch development
High autonomy and ownership signals a senior, meaningful role
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for non-EU candidates across three jurisdictions
Broad years range (6–15) may indicate undefined seniority level or budget uncertainty
Hybrid policy is 4 days in office — limited remote flexibility
Role title says 'Product Manager' but responsibilities are heavily fund operations and governance — may not suit candidates with traditional software PM backgrounds

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range and autonomy signals; not explicitly stated)

ambiguities: The role title is 'Product Manager' but the responsibilities read more like senior fund operations / governance management with minimal traditional PM (roadmap, discovery, prioritisation) language — archetype confidence is medium rather than high.
Years range of 6–15 is unusually wide and does not cleanly map to a single seniority band; treated as Senior IC to Principal.
AML/KYC handling is listed as a responsibility — unclear whether this requires formal AML certification or is informally coordinated.
